title: gilia ochroleuca subsp. clokeyi
intro: Gilia ochroleuca subsp. clokeyi is a subspecies of the white-haired gilia plant, a type of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gilia ochroleuca subsp. clokeyi include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms of traditional medicine.
